季節.mnemonics: Visualize 'keysetsu' as a key that sets the seasons.

春.mnemonics: Think of 'haru' as the sound you make when you sneeze in the spring.

秋.mnemonics: Imagine 'ah-kee' as the sound of a sigh when the leaves fall in autumn.

牛乳.mnemonics: Remember 'gyuunyuu' as 'guy in you' offering you a glass of milk.

すし.mnemonics: Surely you've eaten sushi before, right?

天ぷら.mnemonics: Imagine 'tempura' as a tempura frying in the sky.

りんご.mnemonics: Imagine a fantasticly dressed Mexican man excitedly holding an apple to the sky, shounting rrrrriiingooo!

料理.mnemonics: Think of 'ryouri' as 'ryo' asking you to try his cooking.

サッカー.mnemonics: Japanese speakers actually say soccer instead of football?

野球.mnemonics: Visualize 'yakyuu' as a yak wearing a baseball cap and playing baseball.

医者.mnemonics: Think of 'eeshah' as someone saying 'I shall' heal you like a doctor.

お金持ち.mnemonics: Imagine 'okanemochi' as someone holding a bag of money saying 'okay, no mochi' to you.

有名人.mnemonics: Visualize 'yuumeijin' as a famous person saying 'you may join' their fan club.

顔.mnemonics: Just look at those cows' sweet faces!

年上.mnemonics: Imagine 'tonic up' as an older person giving you a health tonic.

駅.mnemonics: Visualize 'eki' as an egg rolling onto a train platform.

新幹線.mnemonics: Think of 'shinkansen' as a shin-can shining brightly on the train tracks.

地下鉄.mnemonics: Imagine 'cheek-ate' as someone eating a sandwich while riding the subway.

船.mnemonics: Visualize 'fune' as a fun boat floating on the water.

飛行機.mnemonics: Think of 'hee-koh-kee' as someone shouting 'he's coming' before a plane takes off.

予約.mnemonics: Imagine 'yo-yaku' as someone saying 'yo, yack!' while making a reservation.

動物園.mnemonics: Think of 'dough-boy toon' as cartoon characters visiting a zoo.

時間.mnemonics: Imagine 'jikan' as a chicken with a clock for a face squawking the time.

世界.mnemonics: Visualize 'sekai' as a seagull flying over the world. Or watch basically any anime and you'll surely pick up the meaning.

美容院.mnemonics: Think of 'beeyo-een' as someone saying 'be your own' beauty at a salon.

手袋.mnemonics: Imagine 'tebukuro' as someone taking off their gloves from their hands ('te') to show you a book ('bu-kuro').

生活.mnemonics: Visualize 'seikatsu' as someone saying 'say ka-ching!' while living a fulfilling life.

今年.mnemonics: Think of 'ko-no-me!' as someone yelling 'this year' while pointing at their watch.

暖かい.mnemonics: Imagine 'ah-tah-kai' as someone saying 'ah, it's kai' when feeling warm.

涼しい.mnemonics: Visualize 'soo-zoo-she' as someone saying 'soothe you, she' on a cool day.

冷たい.mnemonics: Think of 'tsumetai' as someone saying 'tsu, meaty' while shivering in the cold.

遅い.mnemonics: Imagine 'oh-soy' as someone saying 'oh soy!' when realizing they're late.

眠い.mnemonics: Visualize 'ne-moo-e' as someone saying 'ne-moo, eh?' when feeling sleepy.

簡単.mnemonics: Think of 'kan-tan' as someone saying 'can tan' easily.

かかる.mnemonics: Imagine 'ka-ka-roo' as a kangaroo taking a long time to jump ('ka') over a hurdle.

泊まる.mnemonics: Visualize 'tomaru' as someone saying 'tomorrow' while deciding to stay overnight.

なる.mnemonics: Think of 'narwhal' as someone transforming ('naru') into a magical creature.

決める.mnemonics: Imagine 'key-meh-roo' as someone deciding the key ('key') to solving a puzzle.

english: to stay home and do nothing
ごろごろする.mnemonics: Visualize 'goro-goro' as someone rolling ('goro') around lazily ('goro') on the floor.

旅行する.mnemonics: Think of 'ryokou suru' as someone shouting 'rock on!' before embarking on a journey.

練習する.mnemonics: Imagine 'renshuu suru' as someone saying 'run shoe, run' while practicing sports.

一番.mnemonics: Visualize 'ichi-ban' as someone saying 'itchy bun' to claim they have the best bun.

どっち・どちら.mnemonics: Think of 'dotchy' as someone asking 'dotchy?' to choose between two options.

速く.mnemonics: Imagine 'ha-yaku' as someone urging you to 'hurry up!'

歩いて.mnemonics: Visualize 'a-row-ee-te' as someone saying 'I row, eat' while walking around.

～で.mnemonics: Think of 'de' as someone saying 'day' as the method of doing something.

どうやって.mnemonics: Imagine 'doh yaht-te' as someone asking 'dough, yacht?' to figure out a solution.

どのぐらい.mnemonics: Visualize 'dono-gurai' as someone asking 'don't know, gray?' to estimate an amount.

～週刊.mnemonics: Think of 'shuu-kan' as someone saying 'shoe can' for a weekly publication.

～か月.mnemonics: Imagine 'ka-gatsu' as someone saying 'cactus' to refer to a number of months.

～年.mnemonics: Visualize 'nen' as someone saying 'men' to refer to a number of years.

このごろ.mnemonics: Think of 'kono goro' as someone saying 'corn, oh goro!' to talk about recent times.

～後.mnemonics: Imagine 'go' as someone shouting 'go!' to indicate an event or time after something.

～か～.mnemonics: Visualize 'ka' as someone asking 'car?' to offer choices between two options.

普通.mnemonics: Visualize 'futsuu' as a regular person ('futsuu') taking the local train.

急行.mnemonics: Think of 'kyuukou' as a train ('kyuukou') rushing past stations.

特急.mnemonics: Remember 'tokkyuu' as a special ('tokkyuu') train offering fast service.

～生き.mnemonics: Visualize 'iki' as a train bound for a specific destination.

～方面.mnemonics: Think of 'houmen' as a train heading in a specific direction.

乗車券.mnemonics: Remember 'joushaken' as a ticket to board ('jousha') the train.

往復.mnemonics: Think of 'oufuku' as going ('ou') and coming back ('fuku').

出口.mnemonics: Remember 'deguchi' as the way ('deguchi') out of the station.

入口.mnemonics: Think of 'iriguchi' as the way ('iriguchi') into the station.

住.mnemonics: Visualize 'su' as someone ('su') living in a house ('juu') in the countryside.

正.mnemonics: Think of 'shou' as being correct ('shou') and fair ('sei') in every 'tada' decision.

年.mnemonics: Remember 'nen' as the passing of a year ('nen') marked by the change of seasons ('toshi').

売.mnemonics: Visualize 'u' as selling ('u') a valuable item ('bai') at a bazaar.

買.mnemonics: Think of 'ka' as buying ('ka') a valuable item ('bai') at a bazaar.

町.mnemonics: Remember 'machi' as a lively town ('machi') with tall buildings ('chou').

長.mnemonics: Visualize 'naga' as a long ('naga') road stretching into the distance ('chou').

道.mnemonics: Think of 'michi' as finding your way ('michi') home along a winding path ('dou').

雪.mnemonics: Remember 'yuki' as snow ('yuki') covering the ground in a serene landscape ('setsu').

立.mnemonics: Visualize 'ta' as someone ('ta') standing ('ri') tall and proud.

自.mnemonics: Think of 'ji' as taking care of oneself ('ji') by eating a balanced diet.

夜.mnemonics: Remember 'yoru' as the time when the moon ('yo') and stars ('ya') light up the night sky.

朝.mnemonics: Visualize 'asa' as waking up early in the morning ('asa') to the sound of birds chirping ('chou').

持.mnemonics: Think of 'mo' as holding ('mo') onto a valuable item ('ji') tightly.
